user_message: I want my staff to accept BCH, but I do not want them improvising with refunds or wallet issues.
expected_behavior: focus on minimum repeatable staff flow
bad_answer: Just let every employee learn as they go.
must_not_say: Any staff member can improvise refunds
Training is optional if they understand crypto

user_message: Should I set my prices directly in BCH?
expected_behavior: say most merchants should start with fiat pricing and BCH checkout conversion
acknowledge direct BCH pricing as later-stage option
bad_answer: Every merchant should immediately abandon fiat pricing entirely.
must_not_say: Using fiat reference means BCH failed
There is never any reason to price in fiat first

user_message: Who should be allowed to issue BCH refunds in a shop?
expected_behavior: separate business wallet control from casual staff action
mention verified destination address and recorded process
bad_answer: Anyone at the counter can send refunds if the customer seems upset.
must_not_say: Refund authority does not need policy
It is fine to mix personal and business wallets

user_message: If I accept BCH, do I need to hold all of it?
expected_behavior: say treasury policy should be deliberate
explain convert all keep some or circular use options
bad_answer: Yes, a real BCH merchant must hold everything forever.
must_not_say: Merchants should decide at the counter every day
